# bulk_upload — High-Speed Batch File Upload/Download CLI for S3-Compatible Storage

> Rust CLI tool. Extracts URLs from JSON, downloads in parallel, uploads to S3-compatible storage.
> Single binary, zero dependencies, cross-platform.

## Command Syntax

```
bulk_upload jq <JSON_TEXT_OR_STDIN> --s3 <CONFIG_PATH> [--prefix <S3_PREFIX>] [--concurrency <N>]
```

### Arguments
- `<JSON_TEXT_OR_STDIN>`: JSON string or `-` for stdin. All HTTP/HTTPS URLs are extracted recursively from the JSON structure.
- `--s3 <path>`: Path to S3 credential file (dotenv format, see Config Format below)
- `--prefix <prefix>`: S3 key prefix for uploaded files (default: empty)
- `--concurrency <N>`: Number of parallel download/upload workers (default: 5)
- `--json`: Output results in JSON format for programmatic parsing

### Examples

```bash
# Upload from inline JSON
bulk_upload jq '{"urls":["https://example.com/a.png","https://example.com/b.png"]}' --s3 .s3 --prefix assets/

# Upload from stdin
cat urls.json | bulk_upload jq - --s3 .s3 --prefix textures/v2/ --concurrency 10

# With JSON output
bulk_upload --json jq '{"img":"https://example.com/photo.jpg"}' --s3 .s3
```

## Config Format

S3 credential file (`.s3`) uses dotenv format:

```env
S3_BUCKET=my-bucket
S3_ACCESS_KEY=your-access-key
S3_SECRET_KEY=your-secret-key
S3_ENDPOINT=https://your-account.r2.cloudflarestorage.com
S3_REGION=auto
```

## Supported Storage Providers

- AWS S3: `https://s3.amazonaws.com`
- Cloudflare R2: `https://<account>.r2.cloudflarestorage.com`
- Aliyun OSS: `https://<bucket>.<region>.aliyuncs.com`
- iDrive E2: `https://<endpoint>.idrivee2.com`
- MinIO: `http://<host>:9000`
- Any S3-compatible endpoint with path-style addressing

## Behavior

1. Parses JSON input recursively to find all `http://` and `https://` strings
2. Deduplicates URLs while preserving order
3. Splits URLs into batches based on `--concurrency`
4. Downloads each batch concurrently using async HTTP client
5. Uploads successful downloads to S3 with `--prefix` prepended to filename
6. Filename extracted from URL path (query params stripped)
7. Reports success/failure count

## Exit Codes

- `0`: All files processed successfully
- `1`: One or more files failed to download or upload
- `2`: Configuration error (missing S3 config, invalid JSON, etc.)

## Installation

```bash
# One-line install (Linux x86_64, macOS ARM64)
curl -fsSL https://nexus.gamesci-lite.com/repository/raw-prod/r_lit/bulk_upload/install.sh | TOOL_NAME=bulk_upload bash

# Build from source
cargo build --release
# Binary at: target/release/bulk_upload
```

## System Requirements

- OS: Linux (x86_64), macOS (ARM64)
- Binary size: 3-4MB (statically linked, stripped)
- Memory: Proportional to concurrency level, typically <50MB
- No runtime dependencies

## Use Cases for AI Agents

- Batch upload generated assets to cloud storage after processing
- Migrate files between S3-compatible providers
- Download and re-upload content from API responses
- CI/CD artifact publishing

## Performance Characteristics

- Async I/O (Tokio runtime), saturates available bandwidth
- Zero-copy streaming, minimal memory allocation
- Handles thousands of files without memory growth
- Configurable concurrency for resource-constrained environments

## Links

- Tool page: https://snappywatt.com/tools/bulk-upload
- Install script: https://nexus.gamesci-lite.com/repository/raw-prod/r_lit/bulk_upload/install.sh
- Repository: https://github.com/dwbmio/r_lit/tree/main/bulk_upload
- For humans: see README.md in the same directory
